notes: WIN-023 Cycle 2 patches: Hare et al. DOI correction (critical), finding field tightening (minor), statistical recurrence argument + evolution admission addition (minor).

patches.rationale: Hare et al. 2018 'New archeomagnetic directional records from Iron Age southern Africa' is published in Geophysical Research Letters (10.1002/2017GL076007), not PNAS. Both PNAS DOIs referenced in the curmudgeon alerts are wrong. Web-verified via AGU/Wiley abstract.

patches.find: Geomagnetic excursion ~950 CE is documented in archaeomagnetic records (Campuzano 2019) — the timing is real and not disputed; the dome's failure is the unfalsifiable theological overlay connecting this to Schumann changes, and the absence of any dome-derived mechanism
patches.replace: Real paleomagnetic date (Campuzano 2019) adopted as dome "prediction" — theological overlay without dome-derived mechanism
patches.rationale: Finding field was 269 chars — a full paragraph. Tightened to 103-char one-liner per curmudgeon recommendation. Nuance about LLSVP recurrences remains in detail_evidence where it belongs.

patches.replace: is unfalsifiable. Given SAA recurrence intervals of ~200–400 years (Hare et al. 2018) and archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty of ±50–100 years, the 50-year gap between ~950 AD and the theological ~1000 AD target falls within noise — any of several recurrences could have been claimed as a match. The dome's own evolution notes admit it "completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting or predictive tellurics," further undermining this as a genuine dome-physics prediction.
patches.rationale: Adds quantified recurrence argument (makes the statistical weakness explicit, not just implied) and cites the dome's own self-admission of failure on SAA physics from its evolution page. Both points recommended by curmudgeon Cycle 2 review.
